game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Sushi Cat**: Players initiate each level by tapping the top of the screen to drop Sushi Cat from a chosen horizontal position into a vertical, physics-based environment filled with static sushi pieces and various obstacles. As Sushi Cat descends, he bounces off objects and consumes any sushi he touches, incrementally filling a "belly meter" displayed at the bottom of the screen and visually growing larger, which slightly alters his collision physics. Players are granted a limited number of drops per level, typically 3-5, and must strategically place each drop to maximize sushi collection and fill the belly meter to 100% to win. Progression involves completing 27 main levels and 3 bonus levels, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex obstacle layouts, fewer available sushi pieces, and higher belly meter requirements. Failing to fill the belly meter within the allotted drops results in a level restart.
game_info.description: Drop Sushi Cat through a physics playground filled with delicious sushi! Position your cat at the top of the screen and watch as he bounces through obstacles, collecting sushi to fill his belly meter. You have limited drops per level - use them wisely to reach 100% fullness and advance through increasingly challenging layouts. Collect all the sushi you can to win!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (LEFT/RIGHT): Move drop position horizontally at the top of the screen
SPACE: Drop Sushi Cat from current position
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause during gameplay
R: Restart to title screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic drop mechanics and sushi collection. Verifies that Sushi Cat can be dropped, falls with physics, collides with obstacles and sushi pieces, and that the belly meter increases when collecting sushi.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Drop the cat from different horizontal positions (left, center, right) to test physics interactions. Allow the cat to fall through the level and collect various sushi pieces. Monitor belly meter progress and verify collision responses.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The cat should drop smoothly, bounce realistically off obstacles and walls, collect sushi on contact (which disappears), and the belly meter should increase proportionally. The cat's size should grow slightly as sushi is collected. Test passes if physics and collection mechanics work without errors.

The cat's size should grow slightly as sushi is collected. Test passes if physics and collection mechanics work without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by automatically filling the belly meter to 100%. Simulates optimal drops that collect maximum sushi to trigger the level complete state and verify proper transition to the next level.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automatically drop the cat from calculated optimal positions that maximize sushi collection. Force collect enough sushi to reach 100% belly meter within the allowed drops. Verify transition to win state and level progression.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The belly meter should reach 100%, triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN state. A victory message should display with the option to restart. The game should handle the win condition correctly without errors. Test passes when win state is properly triggered and displayed.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition by exhausting all drops without filling the belly meter. Verifies that the game properly tracks remaining drops and transitions to lose state when drops run out before reaching 100% fullness.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Make drops that intentionally miss most sushi pieces or drop from poor positions. Ensure all drops are used without reaching 100% belly meter. Verify the game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE state.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: After using all drops without reaching 100%, the game should display a lose message. The player should be prompted to restart with 'R'. Test passes if lose condition triggers correctly and game state is properly managed.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing pause functionality during active gameplay. Verifies that ESC key properly pauses physics, rendering, and game logic, then resumes correctly when unpaused.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Drop the cat, then immediately press ESC to pause mid-fall. Verify that physics stops, the cat freezes in position, and a pause overlay appears. Press ESC again to unpause and verify gameplay resumes from the exact same state.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: When paused, all physics and movement should stop completely. The pause overlay should be visible. When unpaused, the cat should continue falling from the same position with the same velocity. Test passes if pause/unpause works seamlessly without state corruption.

TEST_4.expected: When paused, all physics and movement should stop completely. The pause overlay should be visible. When unpaused, the cat should continue falling from the same position with the same velocity. Test passes if pause/unpause works seamlessly without state corruption.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. Verifies that completing a level advances to the next with updated obstacle layouts and sushi arrangements.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Complete the first level by reaching 100% belly meter. Verify transition to level 2 with a new layout. Check that level number increments and the belly meter resets appropriately.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Upon winning level 1, the game should load level 2 with a different obstacle configuration. The level counter should increment, drops should reset, and the belly meter should start at 0%. Test passes if level progression works smoothly.

TEST_5.expected: Upon winning level 1, the game should load level 2 with a different obstacle configuration. The level counter should increment, drops should reset, and the belly meter should start at 0%. Test passes if level progression works smoothly.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing cat growth mechanics and physics changes. Verifies that as Sushi Cat collects more sushi, his visual size increases and his collision radius changes, affecting gameplay.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Systematically collect sushi pieces and observe the cat's visual growth. Monitor changes in bounce behavior and collision interactions as the cat gets larger. Verify smooth scaling throughout the collection process.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: The cat should grow proportionally to sushi collected (up to a reasonable maximum). The Matter.js body should update to match the visual size. Larger size should result in different bounce dynamics and collision behavior. Test passes if growth is smooth and physics adapt correctly.
TEST_6.description: Testing cat growth mechanics and physics changes. Verifies that as Sushi Cat collects more sushi, his visual size increases and his collision radius changes, affecting gameplay.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing boundary conditions and edge cases including dropping at extreme positions, multiple rapid drops, and collision with level boundaries.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Drop the cat from the far left edge, far right edge, and attempt to drop multiple times rapidly. Test collision with all level boundaries (walls, floor). Verify no out-of-bounds errors or physics glitches occur.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: The cat should be contained within the canvas boundaries at all times. Edge drops should work identically to center drops. Rapid drop attempts should be handled gracefully (one drop at a time). Test passes if all boundary conditions are handled without errors or visual glitches.
